gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: Website listed for Office of Coast Survey

gmd:metadataStandardName.gco:CharacterString: ISO 19115-2 Geographic Information - Metadata Part 2 Extensions for imagery and gridded data

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: Link to Geographic Markup Language (GML) description of reference system.

gmd:name.gco:CharacterString: European Petroleum Survey Group Geodetic Parameter Registry

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: Registry that accesses the EPSG Geodetic Parameter Dataset, which is a structured dataset of Coordinate Reference Systems and Coordinate Transformations.

gml:VerticalCS.gml:name: Vertical CS. Axis: height (H). Orientation: up. UoM: foot [FT_US].
gml:VerticalCS.gml:remarks: Used in vertical coordinate reference systems.

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: View the complete metadata record on InPort for more information about this dataset.

gmd:name.gco:CharacterString: NOAA National Bathymetric Source Data AWS bucket

gmd:abstract.gco:CharacterString:
BlueTopo is a compilation of the nation's best available bathymetric data. Created as part of the NOAA Office of Coast Survey's nautical charting mission and its National Bathymetric Source (NBS) program, BlueTopo is curated bathymetric source data to provide a definitive nationwide model of the seafloor and navigational lakes. In the same way that a topographic map details the height of land, BlueTopo details the depth of lake beds and seafloor beneath navigationally significant U.S. waters.
Primary sources of bathymetry in NBS compilations include NOAA and U.S. Army Corps of Engineers hydrographic surveys and topographic bathymetric (topo-bathy) lidar (light detection and ranging) data. Data submitted through the NOAA Office of Coast Survey's external source data process are also included. Coverage gaps in water over 200 meters are filled in BlueTopo through Global Multi-Resolution Topography, a merged model of bathymetry made available through Lamont-Doherty Earth Observatory. In addition to compiled bathymetry, BlueTopo contains metadata with quality metrics from the source data to enable the appropriate use of the product. This dataset has been cut into 1 tiles of size up to 204.9999999999999 columns and 126.99999999999893 and may have been mosaiced, reprojected, and clipped from the original tiles.The data set was generated from a larger data set and includes all valid data within the requested geographic bounds.

gmd:purpose.gco:CharacterString: Created as part of the NOAA Office of Coast Survey's nautical charting mission and its National Bathymetric Source (NBS) program, BlueTopo is curated bathymetric source data to provide a definitive nationwide model of the seafloor and navigational lakes. In the same way that a topographic map details the height of land, BlueTopo details the depth of lake beds and seafloor beneath navigationally significant U.S. waters.

gmd:credit.gco:CharacterString: Source information is included with each file.

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: Website listed for Office of Coast Survey

gmd:keyword.gco:CharacterString: EARTH SCIENCE > OCEANS > BATHYMETRY/SEAFLOOR TOPOGRAPHY > BATHYMETRY

gmd:title.gco:CharacterString: Global Change Master Directory (GCMD) Science Keywords

gmd:keyword.gco:CharacterString: DOC/NOAA/NOS/OCS > Office of Coast Survey, National Ocean Service, NOAA, U.S. Department of Commerce

gmd:title.gco:CharacterString: Global Change Master Directory (GCMD) Data Center Keywords

gmd:otherConstraints.gco:CharacterString: This data set was compiled dynamically. It will be removed 10 days after 2026-01-30 from the NOAA system. Cite As: Office of Coast Survey, [Date of Access]: BlueTopo [Data Date Range], https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/inport/item/75311.

gmd:useLimitation.gco:CharacterString: NOAA provides no warranty, nor accepts any liability occurring from any incomplete, incorrect, or misleading data, or from any incorrect, incomplete, or misleading use of the data. It is the responsibility of the user to determine whether or not the data is suitable for the intended purpose.

gmd:otherConstraints.gmx:Anchor: These data were produced by NOAA and are not subject to copyright protection in the United States. NOAA waives any potential copyright and related rights in these data worldwide through the Creative Commons Zero 1.0 Universal Public Domain Dedication (CC0-1.0).
SPDX License: Creative Commons Zero v1.0 Universal (CC0-1.0)

gmd:otherConstraints.gco:CharacterString: This data set was compiled dynamically. It will be removed 10 days after 2026-01-30 from the NOAA system. Access Constraints: Unrestricted

This data set was compiled dynamically. It will be removed 10 days after 2026-01-30 from the NOAA system. Use Constraints: Data use constraints for BlueTopo vary as it is derived from multiple source datasets. Please check the source data layer for specific data use constraints.
This data set was compiled dynamically. It will be removed 10 days after 2026-01-30 from the NOAA system. Distribution Liability: Creative Commons - CC0-1.0

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Data Management Plan for this record on InPort.
Website listed for Office of Coast Survey
BlueTopo is available to view and access through nowCOAST. The nowCOAST map viewer makes BlueTopo available through a web map tile service and the viewer

Three-layer GeoTIFFs with elevation, uncertainty, contributor information, and raster attribute table (RAT) layers. All layers are presented as 32-bit floating point values. Available to download through NOAA's Open Data Dissemination Program at Amazon Web Services bucket.

gmd:statement.gco:CharacterString: BlueTopo is available for download as three layer GeoTIFFs with elevation, uncertainty, and raster attribute table (RAT) layers. All layers are presented as 32-bit floating point values. The bathymetry is compiled from multiple sources with varying quality and includes forms of interpolation. The bathymetric quality and interpolation is described through the vertical uncertainty layer and the associated data quality raster attribute table.

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: Data were clipped to the user input bounding box. The original BlueTopo rasters are 3-band floating point with bands elevation, uncertainty, and spatial metadata. These have been split into three separate rasters with _elev, _uncert, and _meta suffixes for elevation, uncertainty, and metadata bands respectively. The metadata band is now an integer raster. This was done to facilitate typical user workflows that primarily use the elevation band. If multiple horizontal input files were involved, the highest resolution was used for output. If resampling was needed, the methods used, in band order, were bilinear,bilinear,nearest. Reprojected to spatial reference system EPSG:2236 using GDAL version 3.12.1. Tiles were generated with a target of 12910 pixels per side.
